titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Henderson Todd to J. M.Campbell, 28 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Henderson Todd

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Robert Henderson Todd (b. 1851), crown solicitor for the county and city of Londonderry, to James Henry Musen Campbell. In this letter Todd requests leave for a holiday in Buxton. He states that there is no pending criminal business for the County or City of Londonderry and that his leave would not be in conflict with the interests of his duty. He writes that his partner would be present during his leave and would inform him of any criminal activity during his leave.In April 1916, James Henry Musen Campbell (1851 - 1931) was appointed attorney general for Ireland. His formal re-election as MP, which was then necessary on assuming office, took place on Easter Monday 1916 as the Easter rising broke out.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13162

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Todd && Mark
Solicitors
Robert Henderson Todd M A L L D
Crown Solicitor for County
and City of Londonderry
John Martin Mark
Telephone No 149
6 Shipquay Street Londonderry 28th July 1916 Dear Sir, If you will be good enough to permit me, I propose
going to Buxton on Monday for the month of August. There is no criminal business for the County or City
of Londonderry pending at present requiring my presence, and I do
not anticipate that my personal attendance will be requisite during
the month of August. My Partner will be here during the month, and I have
arranged that any criminal business coming in will be promptly
attended to and that I shall be at once informed of any case arising
during the month. If necessary, I shall, on receipt of a wire, be here
next day. I trust you will be good enough to permit me to take
this holiday, which I very much require. Yours faithfully, R H Todd The Rt. Hon. J. M. Campbell K. C., M.P Attorney General for Ireland, The Castle, Dublin. Request approved
Initialled 29-7-16
Noted W P H 1/8
Noted initialled 1/8
Registered 31-7-16
